purpose: Runtime process-group evidence for the updated 4x/8x TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4-style canary objective.

runtime.parallel_dims_signature_note: Image-bundled TorchTitan ParallelDims requires etp and uses legacy mesh names such as dp_shard_cp and ep.

interpretation.ascend_mapping: This confirms the Ascend-reported FSDP/EP shared-ProcessGroup risk exists in the active image-backed TorchTitan runtime, at least for the 4x FSDP4/EP4 slice.
interpretation.performance_risk: FSDP all-gather/reduce-scatter and EP all-to-all can contend on the same ProcessGroup/stream domain unless the runtime is patched or moved to a TorchTitan path with explicit PG isolation.
not_yet_proven: actual overlap/serialization timing from RCCL or rocprof traces
whether graph-trainer process-group isolation can be enabled safely on this Primus image path

launcher_adjustments_validated.four_gpu_visible_slice: CANARY_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3,4,5 with only CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES works in the image.
launcher_adjustments_validated.rocr_hip_visible_devices: ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES/HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3,4,5 caused PyTorch HIP device-ordinal failures in this image and should not be used for this launcher.
launcher_adjustments_validated.idle_guard: The guard passes for visible GPUs 2,3,4,5 and refuses visible GPUs 0,1 where the existing workload is running.

next_actions: Patch or select a TorchTitan runtime path that gives FSDP and EP distinct ProcessGroups for the canary.
Rerun pg-probe after the patch and require fsdp_group_is_ep_group=false on every rank.
Then run a short 4x or 8x RCCL/rocprof communication trace to measure FSDP and EP overlap/serialization.
